title: Manitoba - 2025 Income tax package - Canada.ca
content:
## Manitoba - 2025 Income tax package
You may be looking for:
- Filing a paper tax return
- Filing a return with tax software
If you are filing a return with tax software, you do not have to get the income tax package. Use the income tax package only if you are filing a paper return or need tax forms or schedules.

## Using the income tax package
The tax package includes both the federal tax return and the provincial form , as well as other forms and schedules that may apply to you.
- Download or view your return and provincial forms Federal tax Income Tax and Benefit Return This is the main form you need to complete your taxes Manitoba tax Form MB428 - Manitoba Tax Calculate the Manitoba tax and credits to report on your return If you received business income from outside Manitoba If you resided in Manitoba on December 31, 2025 (or the date you left Canada if you emigrated in 2025) and all or part of your 2025 business income was earned and can be allocated to a permanent establishment outside Manitoba, use Form T2203, Provincial and Territorial Taxes for Multiple Jurisdictions instead of Form MB428. Information and worksheets to help complete your federal return and provincial form Federal tax Federal income tax and benefit information Find out what's new for 2025 and get help completing your federal return Federal Worksheet Use this worksheet to calculate some of the amounts to report on your return Manitoba tax Manitoba Tax Information Find out what's new for residents of Manitoba for 2025 and get help completing your Manitoba tax and credits form Worksheet MB428 - Manitoba Calculate some of the amounts to report on your Manitoba tax and credits form

### Download or view your return and provincial forms
- Income Tax and Benefit Return This is the main form you need to complete your taxes
- Form MB428 - Manitoba Tax Calculate the Manitoba tax and credits to report on your return If you received business income from outside Manitoba If you resided in Manitoba on December 31, 2025 (or the date you left Canada if you emigrated in 2025) and all or part of your 2025 business income was earned and can be allocated to a permanent establishment outside Manitoba, use Form T2203, Provincial and Territorial Taxes for Multiple Jurisdictions instead of Form MB428.
If you resided in Manitoba on December 31, 2025 (or the date you left Canada if you emigrated in 2025) and all or part of your 2025 business income was earned and can be allocated to a permanent establishment outside Manitoba, use Form T2203, Provincial and Territorial Taxes for Multiple Jurisdictions instead of Form MB428.
- Federal income tax and benefit information Find out what's new for 2025 and get help completing your federal return
- Federal Worksheet Use this worksheet to calculate some of the amounts to report on your return
- Manitoba Tax Information Find out what's new for residents of Manitoba for 2025 and get help completing your Manitoba tax and credits form
- Worksheet MB428 - Manitoba Calculate some of the amounts to report on your Manitoba tax and credits form
- Choose other forms and schedules that may apply Depending on your tax situation, you may need additional forms, schedules, or publications to help complete your tax return . Federal Schedule 2 - Federal Amounts Transferred from your Spouse or Common-Law Partner Schedule 3 - Capital Gains (or Losses) Schedule 5 - Amounts for Spouse or Common-Law Partner and Dependants Schedule 6 - Canada Workers Benefit Schedule 7 - RRSP, PRPP and SPP Unused Contributions, Transfers, and HBP or LLP Activities Schedule 8 - Canada Pension Plan Contributions and Overpayment Schedule 9 - Donations and Gifts Schedule 11 - Federal Tuition Amount and Canada Training Credit Schedule 12 - Multigenerational Home Renovation Tax Credit Schedule 13 - Employment Insurance Premiums on Self-Employment and Other Eligible Earnings Schedule 15 - FHSA Contributions, Transfers and Activities Manitoba Schedule MB428–A - Manitoba Family Tax Benefit Form MB479 - Manitoba Credits Schedule MB(S2) - Provincial Amounts Transferred From Your Spouse or Common-Law Partner Schedule MB(S11) - Manitoba Tuition and Education Amounts

### Choose other forms and schedules that may apply
Depending on your tax situation, you may need additional forms, schedules, or publications to help complete your tax return .
- Schedule 2 - Federal Amounts Transferred from your Spouse or Common-Law Partner
- Schedule 3 - Capital Gains (or Losses)
- Schedule 5 - Amounts for Spouse or Common-Law Partner and Dependants
- Schedule 6 - Canada Workers Benefit
- Schedule 7 - RRSP, PRPP and SPP Unused Contributions, Transfers, and HBP or LLP Activities
- Schedule 8 - Canada Pension Plan Contributions and Overpayment
- Schedule 9 - Donations and Gifts
- Schedule 11 - Federal Tuition Amount and Canada Training Credit
- Schedule 12 - Multigenerational Home Renovation Tax Credit
- Schedule 13 - Employment Insurance Premiums on Self-Employment and Other Eligible Earnings
- Schedule 15 - FHSA Contributions, Transfers and Activities
- Schedule MB428–A - Manitoba Family Tax Benefit
- Form MB479 - Manitoba Credits
- Schedule MB(S2) - Provincial Amounts Transferred From Your Spouse or Common-Law Partner
- Schedule MB(S11) - Manitoba Tuition and Education Amounts

### Give feedback about this page
Thank you for your feedback.
